title: Lake Serene
author: Trip Report JWATERBOY12100
snow: Intermittent snow - not hard to cross
report_text: Arrived to the trailhead at 10 and was the 6th car at the trailhead on this rainy/snowy day. It was a wet one today for sure. The first mile you see parts that have been washed out by flooding and also just annoying downed trees. Unfortunately some stairs have been damaged as well but pretty easy to manage around. The last half mile to maybe a little bit more had snow but didn’t end up pulling out the spikes today and actually forgot the poles at home. The lake was cloudy and moody and snowy and just peaceful. Ended up making the little trip to lunch rock and probably isn’t the smartest choice especially when there wasn’t much views. Didn’t go to Bridal Veil but did make a short little trip to another sweet spot with a waterfall. Round trip on this wet day was 8 miles and 2,420 feet elevation gain.

title: Blue Lake,Rainy Lake,Cutthroat Pass via Cutthroat Lake,Cutthroat Pass via the Pacific Crest Trail,Heather - Maple Pass Loop,Easy Pass,Hart's Pass to Rainy Pass&Pacific Crest Trail (PCT) - Hart's Pass to Holman Pass
author: Trip Report AbamWTA Member100
snow: Snowfields to cross - could be difficult
report_text:
April 3: Summary: Progress from eastside, reached the Liberty Bells, snow depth significantly increased, progress has slowed.

Progress from westside: Rockslide at milepost 130 has not been cleared, a contract to clear it has not been let at this time.

April 30: Details

From Silver Star Gate: " Crews have reached Liberty Bells slide at milepost 163.2 on the second week of spring clearing of State Route 20 North Cascades Highway. From the supervisor report: CR-12 slide was extremely dirty and rocky, requiring significant loader and excavator work through the slide area."

From Newhalem/Diablo: "The road remains closed on the westside at milepost 130 due to rockslide. East of that, multiple washouts have been identified for repairs between milepost 143-146. The rockslide must be cleared before work can start on the washouts. There is currently no estimated time to reopen.

March 30. From the Methow Valley News: "Harts Pass Road is closed from Forest Service Road 5400015 (Monument Creek Trailhead) to Harts Pass, restricting access to the Robinson, Rattlesnake, Harts Pass, Brown Bear, Slate Pass and Slate Peak trailheads. The closure also impacts access to the Ballard, Riverbend, Harts Pass and Meadows campgrounds." Goal is to have road repaired by early summer.

In addition, there are closure on the Twisp River Road: "South of the Twisp River, the Twisp River Horsecamp, the War Creek Trailhead and South Creek Trailhead (South) are closed. Access to South Creek Trailhead (North) is still available."

Take note and make sure you contact the USFS District Ranger Offices for closure info.

March 27 . WSDoT Info:

Progress on the eastside towards Washington Pass is now at milepost 165, about a short mile beyond the road to the Cutthroat Lake/Pass Trailhead.

SR 20: Clearing and repairs continue although there was and will be new snow.

March 26 . News from the Methow Valley Newspaper .

Good news : Snow depth measured on Feb. 25 at Washington Pass was 56" (last year 62") and 73" at Rainy Pass.  Last year, 2024-2025, was also considered a low snow year.

Neither Good nor bad : (1) there has been new snow, but not a lot and (2) WSDoT has already been working on the washout just east of the Early Winters Gate (milepost 178).

Bad news: On March 17 a large rock slide closed SR20 (milepost 130 and noted previously and below).  An emergency contract is being prepared to clear that debris. When cleared, WSDoT will deal with a washout between mileposts 142 and 143 (see Figures). Then crews will tackle a washout that took out a section of highway between mileposts 142 and 143.

Important sources of information: ...wsdot.com/Travel/Real-time/Map  and  ...https://www.facebook.com/search/top?q=wsdot .

March 21 .  A WSDoT Facebook Posting stated the following: "Let’s check in on our hibernating friend, SR 20 North Cascades Highway (see WSDoT Photos).

Spring clearing on our iconic pass begins Monday, March 23! The team will start at Early Winters (MP 178) and head west, so if you have a snowmobile parked at the gate, please move them before Monday morning."

Critical until official opening : "Starting Monday, this area is an active work zone Monday-Thursday from 6 a.m.-4:30 p.m. Please do not go behind the closure points during those hours. Our team works hard to maintain a safe operation, and we need everyone to respect that effort."

In addition to the normal snow and avalanche debris removal that occurs every year, there are at least two major, December 7th triggered washouts between where 20 crosses Granite Creek and the East Creek Trailhead.  In addition, there has been a very recent rock slide between the Ross Dam Trailhead and the Diablo Lake Vista point.

Figures provided by WSDoT and Craig Mullen

Photos: https://www.flickr.com/photos/wsdot/albums/72177720332632718/

associated_hikes.hike_name: Cutthroat Pass via the Pacific Crest Trail
Pacific Crest Trail (PCT) - Hart's Pass to Holman Pass

title: Foster and Marsh Island Loop
author: Trip Report Red FlashWTA Member
trail_conditions: Obstacles on trail:Muddy or wet trail.
report_text:
Seattle Olmsted Circuit, Section 3 Loop Option.  Husky Stadium to Marsh and Foster Islands then to visitor center, Azalea Way, Loop Trail and exit up Boyer to enter Interlaken Park but exit before heading up to Volunteer Park. Back onto Boyer turning past St Demitrios Church and finishing over 520 Pedestrian overpass on way back to Husky Stadium

Bridges connecting Marsh and Foster including walkways along shorelines all in good condition, benefiting from new decking in ?January.  Entering from west, initial Marsh walkway underwater with ineffective plank placement.  Improved to simply muddy in 20 m. Remainder of route on islands passable--good given that lake level only an inch or so lower in places.

Trio on private walk started 8am and finished Loop section less than two hours later. Met challenge of sunken passage by balancing alng about 3m of 8by4.  Able to get a little veg support from branches, Walk remainder without challenges and saw bald eagle pair on Foster and enormous next on slight detour into Montlake Playfield.

title: Ebey's Landing
author: Trip Report Eric KatanaboyWTA MemberOutstanding Trip Reporter300
report_text:
We started at the Prairie Overlook trailhead on a blustery, overcast Thursday morning at 9 a.m.  But there was sunshine breaking through to the west over the Olympics, which nicely illuminated the snowy peaks.

Lots of field mustard blooming along the fenceline, and lots of field chickory blooming on the hillsides.  Many puddles in the trail since it had rained hard the previous night, but the trail was in otherwise good condition.

There was lots of beautiful driftwood along the beach.  We saw Surf Scoters, Bald Eagles, Killdeer, and Hudsonian Whimbrel.  We encountered no other hikers.

title: Elbo Creek
author: Trip Report trailtravelers10
snow: Intermittent snow - not hard to cross
report_text:
It is a winter wonderland as you hike farther up the road. I blazed a boot path to the top.:) The snow, I think, would be too soft for snow shoes to help.

Trail conditions:

- Snow free up to the road. I stepped over 3 fallen trees, no problem.

-Turning right from the trail onto the road, snow is intermittent for the first 1/2 mile.

-As you hike farther up the road the snow accumulates between 4-10 inches. So, it becomes a trudge.

- Continuing on the road to the right beyond the gate the snow depth increases a bit, and a little more the last stretch.
